data.title: The US Solomon Islands (SOL) spot ETF saw a total net inflow of $2.1938 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (February 17th, Eastern Time), the total net inflow of SOL spot ETFs was $2.1938 million. The SOL spot ETF with the largest net inflow yesterday was the Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL), with a net inflow of $1.6953 million, bringing its total historical net inflow to $684 million. The second largest was the Fidelity Solana Fund ETF (FSOL), with a net inflow of $498,500, bringing its total historical net inflow to $159 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of SOL spot ETFs is $726 million, with an SOL net asset value ratio of 1.50%, and a cumulative historical net inflow of $877 million.

data.title: The US XRP spot ETF saw zero inflows yesterday, remaining stable.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the XRP spot ETF saw no net inflows or outflows throughout yesterday (February 17th, Eastern Time), remaining unchanged. As of press time, the XRP spot ETF's total net asset value was $1.064 billion, with an XRP net asset ratio of 1.17%, and historical cumulative net inflows reaching $1.229 billion.

data.title: Solana Spot ETFs Experience Significant Inflows on February 17
data.description:
On February 17, Solana spot ETFs saw a substantial net inflow of $2.1938 million, according to Odaily. The Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) led the inflows with $1.6953 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $684 million. The Fidelity Solana Fund ETF (FSOL) followed with a net inflow of $498,500, accumulating a historical total of $159 million.

As of the latest report, the total net asset value of Solana spot ETFs stands at $726 million, with a Solana net asset ratio of 1.50%. The cumulative historical net inflow has reached $877 million.

data.title: Philippines to Issue Large Bonds to Boost Economic Growth
data.description: The Philippines is set to issue large-sized bonds on Wednesday, marking the first such sale since April. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the government's strategy to increase spending in an effort to stimulate economic growth. This move comes as part of a broader initiative to enhance the country's economic performance.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | U.S. Threatens to Withdraw from International Energy Agency Over Climate Advocacy
data.description: The United States has reiterated its intention to leave the International Energy Agency (IEA) unless the organization reduces its emphasis on climate advocacy and prioritizes energy security. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing tensions between the U.S. and the IEA regarding the agency's focus. The U.S. government has expressed concerns that the IEA's current approach may undermine energy security, which it views as a critical issue. This development comes amid broader discussions on balancing climate initiatives with energy needs. The U.S. stance reflects a growing debate on the global stage about the role of international organizations in addressing climate change while ensuring energy stability. The potential withdrawal could have significant implications for international energy policies and cooperation.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Gold Narrative Shifts Amid Dollar Recovery
data.description: Gold prices are facing challenges as the dollar strengthens, impacting the rebound momentum. According to Jin10, investment banks maintain that the long-term outlook for gold remains unchanged despite the lack of bottom-fishing during the price decline. Fundamental factors continue to provide robust support for gold's future trajectory.

data.title: Ford pitches first $30,000 electric truck built on new Universal EV platform to cut costs
data.description:
Ford is putting a $5 billion bet on its next generation of electric vehicles, with a $30,000 electric truck built on a new Universal EV platform. The company said Tuesday that this platform will use a growing technology that Tesla already commercialized in the U. S. with the Cybertruck. This plan comes as electric vehicle costs […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/ford-pitches-first-30000-electric-truck/

data.title: Three leading Japanese securities firms are considering entering the cryptocurrency trading business.
data.description: Three leading Japanese securities firms with a combined market capitalization of approximately $48 billion have indicated they are considering entering the cryptocurrency trading business. Nomura Holdings, Japan's largest securities provider managing approximately $673 billion in client assets, will have its Swiss cryptocurrency subsidiary, Laser Digital, conduct the business. The report states that two other companies—Daiwa Securities Group and SMBC Nikko Securities—are also actively considering entering the cryptocurrency trading market. These three companies anticipate a surge in demand for cryptocurrency investments when Tokyo relaxes restrictions on cryptocurrency exchange-traded funds (ETFs), thus requiring them to plan ahead. (DL News)

data.title: Lyn Alden on Monetary Environment and Investment Strategy
data.description:
Lyn Alden provides insights into the current monetary environment, noting a gradual increase in the Federal Reserve's balance sheet rather than rapid money printing. According to NS3.AI, she discusses the tension between the Federal Reserve's independence and political influence, as well as the evolving global monetary order where gold and Bitcoin are gaining institutional recognition.

Alden highlights the impact of the long-term debt cycle on economic and geopolitical instability. She emphasizes the importance of a multipolar reserve asset system that includes gold and cryptocurrencies. Despite Bitcoin's recent underperformance compared to gold, Alden maintains cautious optimism about its future.

For investors, Alden recommends a diversified portfolio approach. She advises focusing on high-quality equities, hard assets such as Bitcoin and precious metals, and maintaining liquidity to navigate ongoing macroeconomic challenges and technological disruptions.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Highlights Network Layer Anonymity as Key Privacy Concern
data.description:
Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin recently shared insights on the X platform regarding the importance of network layer anonymity in privacy technology. According to ChainCatcher, Buterin emphasized that this aspect has been a missing component in both on-chain privacy solutions like Railgun and off-chain zero-knowledge applications such as ZK API tickets.

Buterin also noted that many applications have low bandwidth requirements but are highly sensitive to latency. Achieving a balance between low latency and high anonymity is crucial for the advancement of related technologies.

data.title: South African Radio Telescope Detects Most Distant Hydroxyl Megamaser
data.description: South African Radio Astronomy Observatory announced on February 17 that astronomers have captured a hydroxyl megamaser over 8 billion light-years away from Earth using the MeerKAT radio telescope. According to Jin10, this discovery marks the most distant hydroxyl megamaser detected to date.

data.title: Japan's Economic Minister Highlights $36 Billion U.S. Investment Projects
data.description: Japan's Economic Minister Akazawa Ryo Masa has announced three significant investment projects in the United States, totaling $36 billion. According to Jin10, these projects include a $600 million investment in synthetic diamond production, a $2.1 billion allocation for an oil project, and a $33.3 billion investment in gas-fired power generation for data centers. The minister emphasized ongoing close coordination with the U.S. regarding the details of these projects.

data.title: Japan Accounting Group Advocates for Relaxed Rules on Insurer Bond Losses
data.description:
Japan's accounting standards body is proposing changes to ease regulations concerning bond losses for insurers. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the group's efforts to address the financial challenges faced by insurance companies due to fluctuating bond markets. The proposed adjustments aim to provide insurers with more flexibility in managing their financial statements, potentially reducing the impact of bond market volatility on their reported earnings.

The move comes as insurers grapple with the effects of rising interest rates, which have led to significant bond losses. The accounting group believes that revising the rules could help stabilize the financial reporting environment for these companies, allowing them to better navigate the current economic landscape.

Industry experts have noted that the proposed changes could have broader implications for the financial sector, potentially influencing how other financial institutions manage similar challenges. The accounting standards body is expected to continue discussions with stakeholders to refine the proposal and ensure it meets the needs of the industry while maintaining transparency and accountability in financial reporting.

The initiative reflects a growing recognition of the need for regulatory frameworks to adapt to changing market conditions, ensuring that financial institutions can maintain stability and resilience in the face of economic uncertainties.

data.title: Cloudflare Confirms No Service Interruptions Amid Reports of Network Issues
data.description: Cloudflare has clarified that there are currently no issues or interruptions affecting its services. According to PANews, this statement comes after DownDetector, a website monitoring network conditions, indicated problems with several online services. Users have reported issues with YouTube, Google, Amazon web services, and Cloudflare. As of now, these reports continue to circulate.

data.title: Trump says tax refunds could soar over 20% thanks to one big beautiful bill
data.description:
President Trump signed the “One Big Beautiful Bill,” which could give Americans bigger tax refunds this year.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/trump-says-tax-refunds-could-soar-over-20/

data.title: Zora launched its attention marketplace on Solana and is recruiting "attention economists."
data.description: Solana announced on the X platform that its decentralized SocialFi platform, Zora, has launched Attention Markets on the Solana blockchain. Traders can trade trending terms, topics, hashtags, and trends, create "Trends" before a trend explodes, and track profits and losses in real time. Zora co-founder Jacob Horne stated that currently, publishing a Trend requires 1 SOL to suppress spam. Trends themselves do not offer creator rewards, but "Pairs" can be created under Trends to receive creator incentives. In addition, Zora has also posted a job advertisement seeking an "Attention Economist," a position responsible for tracking trending topics on platforms such as TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, and X.

data.title: XRP Spot ETF Shows No Net Change on February 17
data.description: XRP Spot ETF experienced no net inflow or outflow throughout February 17, according to ChainCatcher. As of the latest report, the total net asset value of the XRP Spot ETF stands at $1.064 billion, with an XRP net asset ratio of 1.17%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $1.229 billion.

data.title: Hyperliquid Platform Whale Holdings Reach $2.869 Billion
data.description:
Hyperliquid platform whale holdings have reached $2.869 billion, according to ChainCatcher. Data from Coinglass reveals that long positions account for $1.419 billion, representing 49.48% of the total holdings, while short positions amount to $1.449 billion, making up 50.52%. The long positions have incurred a loss of $149 million, whereas the short positions have gained $251 million.

A notable whale address, 0xa5b0..41, has taken a 15x leveraged long position on ETH at a price of $2015.41, currently showing an unrealized loss of $2.0965 million.

data.title: Joseph Lubin Discusses Cryptocurrency Market Outlook and Ethereum's Role
data.description:
Joseph Lubin, founder and CEO of Consensys and co-founder of Ethereum, recently shared his insights on the current state and future trajectory of the cryptocurrency market during an interview in Hong Kong. According to ChainCatcher, Lubin maintains a cautiously optimistic outlook, emphasizing that the digital asset economy is driven by functional utility rather than mere speculation.

Lubin advised against viewing Bitcoin as a traditional safe-haven asset at this stage. He described the broader crypto landscape as akin to a "startup ecosystem," suggesting that positioning Bitcoin as a secure store of value might be premature given the current developmental challenges. In contrast, he highlighted the stronger functional demand for Ethereum's native cryptocurrency, ETH.

Lubin argued that ETH's utility in powering decentralized applications, smart contracts, and broader ecosystem activities gives it an advantage in real-world adoption over Bitcoin's primary narrative. He underscored Ethereum's enduring significance in the evolving financial infrastructure.

He noted that institutional involvement is deepening, with major banks, trading platforms, and financial networks increasingly building on Ethereum-based technologies and second-layer scaling solutions. This institutional momentum, he stated, indicates that despite market volatility, Ethereum is transitioning towards becoming the foundational layer of next-generation finance.

Overall, Lubin's remarks reflect confidence in Ethereum's long-term potential despite short-term market pressures. He portrayed the ecosystem as resilient and innovative, with tools like Ethereum and MetaMask poised to drive meaningful progress in the digital economy.

data.title: Polymarket's 5-Minute Crypto Candles Stir Market Interest
data.description: Crypto KOL Eli5defi on X highlights the growing interest in Polymarket's new 5-minute crypto candles. Enthusiasts are closely monitoring Bitcoin's price movements, speculating on potential increases or decreases within short time frames. This development reflects the dynamic nature of the cryptocurrency market, where traders are keen to capitalize on rapid price changes.
